title: Fermented Silky Tofu Stir-fry
description: A cold-water fermented tofu dish that’s perfect for a sick day in Vietnamese cuisine, featuring low-sodium-friendly preparation.
instructions: In a wok or large skillet, heat vegetable broth over medium-high heat.
Add fermented tofu blocks, cover with white pepper powder and chili flakes if using. Let them sit for about 5 minutes without stirring.
Deglaze the pan with low-sodium soy sauce, ensuring it coats all surfaces of the tofu evenly.
Stir-fry gently until the broth is mostly absorbed and the fermented tofu starts to turn golden brown around the edges (this process may take 10-20 minutes).
Toss in finely chopped green onions and a bit of tumeric for color, continuing to stir-fry for another minute.
Remove from heat and serve hot or at room temperature as a snack.
